mutation_kill_followup.note: mutation-kill (the STZ-designed correctness proxy) does NOT discriminate c5: it ties the truth-best (c5, 1.0) with the truth-WORST (c3, 0.9535). The flagged gap is now closed.

deviation_from_prereg: Still a cron-only, in-sample probe (not the cross-slice train/test, and coverage not separately computed). But the proxy most likely to matter — mutation-kill — is now tested.
verdict: Proxy exhaustion on cron for the FULL computable proxy set {sealed, malformed, codeHealth, mutation-kill}: none ranks the truth-best c5 first; baseline already at the achievable max (0.9767). STRUCTURAL reason (earned, not asserted): all four proxies are SEALED-SUITE-DERIVED, and c5's truth advantage is OUTSIDE the sealed suite by construction (c5 is sealed-WORSE 0.9992 yet truth-BEST 1.0). No sealed-derived weighting can reach it. Since evolution cannot beat baseline in-sample, the cross-slice train/test is moot.
the_real_open_lever: the ONLY selection signal independent of the sealed suite is the JUDGE (LLM reasoning over code+contract). Prior CONTROLS-2 showed a frozen judge picks the spec-correct candidate 3/3. Evolving/testing the judge as a selection gene (rubricId), train/test across slices vs the numeric-proxy baseline, is the honest path to a possible competency positive — NOT more numeric-weight evolution.
